avatarLegendsReleaseDatePlatforms.title: Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game Release Date, Price, and Platforms
avatarLegendsReleaseDatePlatforms.subtitle: The full launch is set for July 23, 2026, with digital pre-orders live on Steam, PlayStation Store, and Xbox / Microsoft Store. The Standard Edition is priced at $29.99, while the Digital Deluxe Edition is priced at $49.99.
avatarLegendsReleaseDatePlatforms.intro: Use this section to check the current launch date, supported platforms, edition prices, and preorder bonuses in one place. July 2-5, 2026 is the closed beta window for eligible pre-orders, while July 23, 2026 is the full release date.

items.label: Full Release Date
items.details: Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game is currently listed for release on July 23, 2026 across the main digital storefronts.

items.label: PC via Steam
items.details: Steam lists the game as a fast-paced 1v1 fighter with hand-drawn 2D animation, proprietary rollback netcode, full cross-play, and pre-purchase options for both Standard and Digital Deluxe editions.

items.label: PlayStation 5
items.details: The PlayStation Store lists the PS5 release for July 23, 2026, with pre-order access, 1-2 player support, optional online play, and PS Plus required for online multiplayer.

items.label: Xbox Series X|S and PC
items.details: The Xbox store lists the game as optimized for Xbox Series X|S with Xbox Play Anywhere, online multiplayer, shared or split-screen play, cloud saves, and cross-platform multiplayer support.

items.label: Nintendo Switch and Switch 2
items.value: Switch listed for July 23, 2026; Switch 2 and physical edition later in 2026
items.details: Current release coverage lists Nintendo Switch alongside PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC for the July 23 digital launch, with Nintendo Switch 2 and physical editions following later in 2026.

items.label: Pre-Order Bonuses
items.value: Closed Beta access, Samurai Appa skin, exclusive colors, and Year 1 Pass vote
items.details: Pre-orders include closed beta access from July 2-5, a Samurai Appa support character skin, gold color variants for Aang, Korra, Zuko, Katara, Toph, and Sokka, plus voting rights for a Year 1 Pass character.

avatarLegendsCharactersRoster.title: Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game Characters and Launch Roster
avatarLegendsCharactersRoster.subtitle: The launch roster includes 12 playable fighters from Avatar: The Last Airbender, The Legend of Korra, and Avatar lore. Several early favorites also receive preorder-exclusive gold color variants.
avatarLegendsCharactersRoster.intro: This roster section helps players quickly scan every launch fighter, their source series, and their combat identity before choosing a main. The game also uses support characters tied to each fighter, giving the same playable character multiple tactical routes.

avatarLegendsBeginnerGuide.title: Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game Beginner Guide
avatarLegendsBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Start with movement, simple attacks, support choices, Flow, and Training Mode before jumping into ranked matches. The game is built to be easy to pick up while still leaving room for serious fighting-game depth.
avatarLegendsBeginnerGuide.intro: This beginner path teaches new players what to learn first without turning the first hour into a vocabulary exam. Follow the steps in order to understand the basic game loop before chasing advanced combos.

items.title: Pick a Fighter You Understand
items.summary: Start with a familiar character such as Aang, Korra, Zuko, Katara, Toph, Sokka, or Azula.
items.details: The game launches with 12 playable characters. Choose one fighter first and stay with them long enough to learn their movement, normal attacks, Flow options, and support choices.

items.title: Choose a Support Character
items.summary: Each fighter can be paired with support characters that add unique abilities.
items.details: Support characters are not just cosmetic extras. They change your tactical options, enhance different strengths, and can help you build a style around pressure, defense, movement, or control.

items.title: Learn Movement and Flow First
items.summary: Flow is the core system for fast movement, evasive actions, and character-specific follow-ups.
items.details: Use Flow to dash, evade, slip under projectiles, launch into the air, or chain into character-specific abilities. Learn this before memorizing long combos, because standing still in a fighting game is a quick way to lose.

items.title: Use Story and Arcade to Learn the Cast
items.summary: Story Mode and Arcade Mode are the safest first stops for casual players.
items.details: Story Mode focuses on the fate of time and space, while Arcade Mode gives every playable character a unique storyline. These modes help you test fighters before committing to online matches.

items.title: Practice in Training Mode
items.summary: Training Mode includes advanced tools such as hitboxes, frame data, and save states.
items.details: Use Training Mode to test attacks, support timing, Flow routes, and Energy usage. This mode is useful for both complete beginners and players who want to study matchups properly.

items.title: Play Offline Versus Before Ranked
items.summary: Try Offline Versus, Casual matches, and lobbies before stepping into Ranked play.
items.details: The game supports Offline and Online Versus, Ranked and Casual matches, lobbies, spectator mode, replays, rollback netcode, and cross-play. Use Casual and lobbies to build match experience before climbing ranked.

items.title: Spend Energy on Big Moments
items.summary: Energy Points power enhanced special moves and cinematic Super Arts.
items.details: Build Energy Points through skilled play, then spend them when an enhanced special move or Super Art can extend pressure, punish a mistake, or close out a round.

avatarLegendsCombatMechanics.title: Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game Combat Mechanics Guide
avatarLegendsCombatMechanics.subtitle: Combat centers on Flow movement, Energy Points, enhanced special moves, cinematic Super Arts, support characters, and distinct bending styles. The result is a simple first layer with enough depth to reward committed players.
avatarLegendsCombatMechanics.intro: This guide breaks down the systems players need to understand before building combos or choosing a main. Start with Flow, then learn how Energy, support characters, and character-specific bending styles shape each matchup.

items.name: Flow Mechanic
items.summary: Flow lets players dodge, slip under projectiles, launch into the air, or chain into character-specific abilities with a single input.
items.beginner_use: Use Flow to escape obvious pressure and move around projectiles.
items.advanced_hook: Each fighter has unique Flow options, so mastering Flow changes from character to character.

items.name: Energy Points
items.summary: Energy Points are built through skilled play and spent on stronger offensive options.
items.beginner_use: Save Energy until you can clearly use it for pressure, punishment, or round-ending damage.
items.advanced_hook: Meter decisions affect whether you play safely, extend offense, or gamble on a comeback.

items.name: Enhanced Special Moves
items.summary: Enhanced special moves use Energy Points to strengthen key attacks and create bigger tactical swings.
items.beginner_use: Use enhanced specials after confirming that your attack is likely to connect or keep pressure.
items.advanced_hook: Enhanced specials can change spacing, pressure timing, and combo routes.

items.name: Super Arts
items.summary: Super Arts are powerful cinematic attacks that can turn the tide of battle when used at the right moment.
items.beginner_use: Do not throw Super Arts randomly. Use them after a punish, combo route, or clear opening.
items.advanced_hook: Super Arts reward meter awareness, matchup knowledge, and clean timing.

items.name: Support Characters
items.summary: Each fighter can choose from three support characters, each adding unique abilities and strategic options.
items.beginner_use: Pick a support that covers what your fighter lacks, such as mobility, pressure, defense, or control.
items.advanced_hook: Support choice can reshape the same fighter into different matchup plans.

items.name: Bending Styles
items.summary: Characters with the same element still play differently, with movesets shaped by their personality, fighting style, and story role.
items.beginner_use: Do not choose a fighter only by element. Test how they move, attack, and use Flow.
items.advanced_hook: Azula uses aerial mobility, traps, Focused Mode, and Firelord Mode, while Sokka plays around weapons, tricky movement, and Flow cancels instead of bending.

items.name: Online Combat
items.summary: Online play includes rollback netcode, cross-play, Ranked and Casual matches, lobbies, spectator mode, and replays.
items.beginner_use: Start in Casual matches or lobbies before playing Ranked.
items.advanced_hook: Replays and spectator mode help players study habits, matchup mistakes, and pressure sequences.

items.name: Training Tools
items.summary: Training Mode includes hitboxes, frame data, save states, and other tools for learning attacks and routes.
items.beginner_use: Use Training Mode to test your safest buttons, anti-air options, and support timing.
items.advanced_hook: Frame data and hitboxes help competitive players refine punishment, spacing, and matchup preparation.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game

questions.question: When is the Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game release date?
questions.answer: Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game is currently listed for a full release on July 23, 2026 across Steam, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Nintendo Switch, with pre-orders open on each storefront.

questions.question: How many playable characters are in Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game?
questions.answer: Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game launches with 12 playable fighters pulled from Avatar: The Last Airbender, The Legend of Korra, and Avatar lore, with five more added through the Year 1 Pass.

questions.question: What platforms support Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game?
questions.answer: Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game supports PC via Steam, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Nintendo Switch for the July 23, 2026 digital launch, with Nintendo Switch 2 and physical editions following later in 2026.

questions.question: Does Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game support crossplay and rollback netcode?
questions.answer: Yes. Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game uses proprietary rollback netcode and supports full crossplay across modern platforms and PC, with Ranked, Casual, lobbies, spectator mode, and replays built in.

questions.question: How much does Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game cost?
questions.answer: Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game is priced at $29.99 for the Standard Edition and $49.99 for the Digital Deluxe Edition, which adds the Year 1 Pass, digital artbook, soundtrack, and unique HUDs.

questions.question: Is there a Closed Beta for Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game?
questions.answer: Yes. Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game runs a Closed Beta from July 2 through July 5, 2026 for eligible pre-orders, featuring Aang, Korra, Katara, Toph, Zuko, Sokka, and Azula.
